design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling rooms, defeat enemies with hair-based melee and chip-based ranged weapons, face boss at end of level, progress through computer virus levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat against mutant creatures spawned by alien computer virus; resource management of chip points for special weapons
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Military operative who has transferred consciousness into binary code, manifesting as kabuki ancestor to combat digital threats from within the defense computer
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unusual protagonist concept (25-year-old military agent appearing as kabuki actor ancestor); unconventional weapon system (long hair as melee weapon combined with chip-based energy attacks); stylized visual identity mixing cyberpunk and traditional Japanese aesthetics

difficulty.checkpointing: Single boss per level; level-based progression with reset on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select accessible via Controller 2 cheat code (A×8, B×4, Right×16) allows players to skip levels and adjust chip levels during debug mode, though not documented as official difficulty modes

progression.upgrade_system: Chip points system funding special weapons (Energy Gun, Fusion Gun, Quantum Bombs, Remote-Controlled Bolo); sixth unused weapon suggests iterative design
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression through computer system; each level culminates in boss encounter
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select enables non-linear progression path
Regional versions contain story differences (Japanese film tie-in narrative removed in international releases)
Sound test accessible via cheat code suggests hidden content discovery

world_building_techniques: Intro cutscene establishing protagonist identity and mission context
Level environment design reflects digital/viral corruption (debris, mutant creatures, parasitic environments)
Final level revelation of alien virus origin from Hyperion probe

Regional narrative differences: Japanese version ties to 1990 Zipang film (protagonist as 15-year-old Bobby Yano transforming into samurai ancestor Jigoku Gokurakumaru); international versions reimagined as standalone military operative Colonel Scott O'Connor story
Year 2056 setting establishes cyberpunk context for consciousness transfer technology

faction_indicators: Earth defense vs. alien intrusion conflict
danger_signals: Computer virus of unknown origin (later revealed as alien)
Biological properties manifested in digital space (debris, mutant creatures, parasites)
Hyperion probe threat to fire laser weapons against human population
environmental_storytelling: Defense computer interior corrupted by viral presence
Visual representation of binary code transformation
Kabuki aesthetic suggesting ancestral connection within digital consciousness
Progressive levels revealing expanding scope of virus threat within computer systems

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Kabuki Quantum Fighter (TCRF)
